decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of 58.6°C, well below the 85°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader conductivity. The current value is at the upper bound (500.0), but the editable variable definition allows tuning within [20.0, 500.0]. Since it is already at the maximum allowed by the schema bounds, no material increase is possible. Instead, I will increase the spreader width to enhance lateral heat spreading towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width maximizes the lateral area available to conduct heat from the chip towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries (25°C), thereby reducing the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance between the chip and the side cooling boundaries.
Lower peak temperature in the chip component.
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader.
risk_notes: The spreader width is approaching the design domain limit (1.2m); ensure it does not exceed bounds in future steps.
Diminishing returns may occur as the spreader approaches the full width of the domain.
Geometric change requires mesh regeneration which might slightly alter numerical precision.
